parameterdescription.para: an int specifying the size in byte to allocate.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the allocated area or NULL in case of lack of memory.

parameterdescription.para: an unsigned int specifying the size in byte to allocate.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the allocated area or NULL in case of lack of memory.

briefdescription.para: a malloc() equivalent, with logging of the allocation info.

parameterdescription.para: an int specifying the size in byte to allocate.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the allocated area or NULL in case of lack of memory.

memberdef.argsstring: (void *ptr, size_t size, const char *file, int line)
memberdef.name: xmlReallocLoc

parameterdescription.para: an int specifying the size in byte to allocate.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the allocated area or NULL in case of lack of memory.

briefdescription.para: a realloc() equivalent, with logging of the allocation info.

parameterdescription.para: an int specifying the size in byte to allocate.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the allocated area or NULL in case of lack of memory.

briefdescription.para: a free() equivalent, with error checking.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new string or NULL if allocation error occurred.

briefdescription.para: a strdup() equivalent, with logging of the allocation info.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new string or NULL if allocation error occurred.
the size of a memory allocation.

briefdescription.para: Provides the amount of memory currently allocated.

simplesect.para: an int representing the amount of memory allocated.

briefdescription.para: Provides the number of memory areas currently allocated.

simplesect.para: an int representing the number of blocks

parameterdescription.para: the amount of memory to dump

xrefdescription.para: This function is a no-op. Call xmlCleanupParser to free global state but see the warnings there. xmlCleanupParser should be only called once at program exit. In most cases, you don't have call cleanup functions at all.

briefdescription.para: Free up all the memory allocated by the library for its own use.

detaileddescription.para: This should not be called by user level code.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlFreeFunc freeFunc, xmlMallocFunc mallocFunc, xmlReallocFunc reallocFunc, xmlStrdupFunc strdupFunc)
memberdef.name: xmlMemSetup

briefdescription.para: Override the default memory access functions with a new set This has to be called before any other libxml routines !

detaileddescription.para: Should this be blocked if there was already some allocations done ?

briefdescription.para: Provides the memory access functions set currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: place to save the free() function in use
place to save the malloc() function in use
place to save the realloc() function in use
place to save the strdup() function in use

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlFreeFunc freeFunc, xmlMallocFunc mallocFunc, xmlMallocFunc mallocAtomicFunc, xmlReallocFunc reallocFunc, xmlStrdupFunc strdupFunc)
memberdef.name: xmlGcMemSetup

briefdescription.para: Override the default memory access functions with a new set This has to be called before any other libxml routines ! The mallocAtomicFunc is specialized for atomic block allocations (i.e.

detaileddescription.para: of areas useful for garbage collected memory allocators
Should this be blocked if there was already some allocations done ?

parameterdescription.para: the malloc() function to use for atomic allocations
